skill.name: longbridge-quant
skill.description: Analyze quantitative trading strategies with Longbridge market data. Covers pairs trading, factor models, statistical tests, optimization, execution costs, hedging, and ML signals.
skill.summary: Build and review quantitative strategy frameworks using Longbridge data and CLI workflows.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found no malicious code, prompt injection, credential theft, or Windows SAM access. Most hits are markdown backticks, financial terms such as in-sample and out-of-sample, or statistical model names. The remaining real risk is user-directed external CLI execution, including optional authenticated portfolio data access, so publication should include a warning.

medium_findings.title: User-Directed External CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run Longbridge CLI commands for market data and quant indicator workflows. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but users should approve commands, symbols, dates, and options before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly document executable CLI commands. The commands are not hidden or obfuscated, but they still invoke local external tooling.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Portfolio Data Access
medium_findings.description: The hedging workflow includes commands for Longbridge positions, which can expose portfolio holdings when the user is logged in with trade scope. No exfiltration path was found, but this needs user consent and data handling care.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly says positions require login with trade scope and shows the positions command. The risk is sensitive financial data access, not command injection or theft.

low_findings.title: Python Package Installation Guidance
low_findings.description: The statistical workflow tells users to install common Python packages. This is normal setup guidance, but package installation changes the local environment and carries normal supply-chain risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The package install commands are explicit and legitimate. Risk is limited because they install named public data-science packages rather than executing opaque scripts.

low_findings.title: Static Critical Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Windows SAM and weak cryptography indicators were not confirmed. The cited text uses financial and statistical terms, including in-sample, out-of-sample, GARCH, bootstrap, and hash-related wording in non-security contexts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows finance and statistics documentation, not credential stores or cryptographic implementation. No malicious intent or sensitive file access was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: Many Ruby shell execution findings are markdown inline-code or fenced-code examples. They are not Ruby backtick execution in source code, although some examples document commands users may choose to run.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are markdown references, and the flagged backticks are formatting markers. No Ruby files or embedded Ruby execution constructs were present in the reviewed skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains shell command examples for Longbridge CLI and Python package installation. Commands are visible and task-related, but they should be treated as user-approved external execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly visible in markdown. They are not hidden payloads, but they do cross the boundary into local command execution.

content.value_statement: Quant research requires repeatable workflows for data, tests, and backtests. This skill guides Longbridge-based analysis for factors, pairs, volatility, hedging, and ML signals.
actual_capabilities: Guides pairs trading and cointegration analysis with hedge ratios and Z-score signals.
Builds multi-factor and factor screening workflows using valuation, momentum, quality, and low-volatility inputs.
Outlines statistical tests such as ADF, GARCH, regression diagnostics, bootstrap, and IC/IR analysis.
Designs strategy optimization, walk-forward validation, and execution cost modelling workflows.
Supports hedging analysis for beta, options protection, tail risk, and cross-asset exposure.
Documents Longbridge CLI usage for K-line data and server-side indicator script runs.
limitations: It provides analysis workflows and examples, not personalized investment advice.
Longbridge CLI or MCP access is needed for live market data retrieval.
Some workflows may require login before viewing portfolio positions.
Backtest and ML results depend on data quality, lookback length, and user assumptions.

use_cases.title: Validate a pairs strategy
use_cases.description: Test whether two symbols are cointegrated, estimate hedge ratio, and define entry and exit thresholds.

use_cases.title: Rank a stock universe
use_cases.description: Combine value, momentum, quality, and low-volatility factors into a transparent scorecard.

use_cases.title: Estimate portfolio hedges
use_cases.description: Review beta exposure, options protection, tail-risk hedges, and estimated hedge costs.

prompt_templates.title: Fetch Market Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Longbridge data to review daily K-line history for TSLA.US over the last 252 trading days. Summarize missing data and basic return statistics.

prompt_templates.title: Test Cointegration
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze NVDA.US and AMD.US as a pairs trade. Check correlation, cointegration, hedge ratio, half-life, Z-score thresholds, and key risks.

prompt_templates.title: Build Factor Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-factor ranking model for an index universe using value, momentum, quality, and low-volatility factors. Include normalization and caveats.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Walk-Forward Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a walk-forward optimization plan for a momentum strategy. Include parameter search, IS/OOS windows, degradation checks, and overfitting warnings.

output_examples.input: Analyze whether NVDA.US and AMD.US are suitable for pairs trading.
output: Correlation and cointegration checks are summarized with test conclusions.
The report estimates hedge ratio, spread Z-score, and mean-reversion half-life.
Entry, exit, stop, and risk notes are presented for review before any trade.

output_examples.input: Build a factor screen for HSI.HK constituents.
output: The screen ranks candidates by value, momentum, quality, and low-volatility scores.
Top and bottom groups are explained with factor attribution.
The output notes missing fields, universe limits, and data-source assumptions.

output_examples.input: Review hedge choices for a concentrated equity portfolio.
output: The response compares beta hedge, protective put, collar, and tail-risk hedge choices.
It estimates protection level, cost, residual exposure, and basis risk.
It includes a clear caveat that hedging reduces risk and can limit upside.

best_practices: Confirm CLI commands and authenticated data access with the user before execution.
Use out-of-sample and walk-forward checks before trusting a backtest result.
State assumptions, data windows, missing values, and investment caveats in every report.
anti_patterns: Do not treat backtest performance as a forecast or guarantee.
Do not run portfolio position commands without explicit user consent.
Do not compare factor scores across universes without consistent normalization.

faq.question: Does this skill place trades?
faq.answer: No. It supports analysis, backtesting, screening, and hedging review. It does not place live orders.

faq.question: Is Longbridge login required?
faq.answer: Public market data workflows may not need login. Portfolio positions and some account data require authentication.

faq.question: What data does it use?
faq.answer: It is designed around Longbridge K-line, option chain, factor, and portfolio data workflows.

faq.question: Can it analyze machine learning strategies?
faq.answer: Yes. It outlines rolling walk-forward workflows using scikit-learn classifiers and engineered market features.

faq.question: What should users verify before running commands?
faq.answer: Users should verify symbols, dates, login scope, output format, and whether portfolio data will be accessed.

faq.question: Does it provide investment advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides analytical frameworks and caveats. Users must make independent investment decisions.
